warm.text: Warm undertone: skin has golden/yellow/peach warmth; veins look green. Flatter with warm, earthy hues - camel, olive, rust, terracotta, mustard, cream, coral, warm reds, bronze. Gold jewelry beats silver. Avoid icy/cool pastels and stark pure white (choose ivory/cream instead).

cool.text: Cool undertone: skin has pink/blue/rosy tones; veins look blue/purple. Flatter with cool, clear hues - navy, emerald, sapphire, jewel tones, true red, cool pinks, lavender, charcoal, pure white. Silver jewelry beats gold. Avoid muddy oranges and warm yellows.

neutral.text: Neutral undertone: a balance of warm and cool; veins look blue-green. Most colors work - lean on soft, slightly muted versions. Great with jade, teal, dusty rose, taupe, denim blue, soft white. Both gold and silver work. Avoid only the most extreme neon/clashing shades.

spring.text: Spring (warm + light + bright): warm, clear, light colors - coral, peach, golden yellow, warm green, camel, ivory, bright aqua. Keep contrast low-to-medium and colors fresh, not muted.

summer.text: Summer (cool + light + soft): soft, cool, muted colors - powder blue, dusty rose, lavender, soft navy, mauve, cool grey, soft white. Avoid heavy black and harsh brights.

autumn.text: Autumn (warm + deep + muted): rich, warm, earthy colors - rust, olive, mustard, terracotta, forest green, camel, cream, bronze, deep teal. Avoid icy pastels and pure white/black.

winter.text: Winter (cool + deep + bright): bold, cool, high-contrast colors - true black, pure white, royal blue, emerald, ruby, magenta, icy pastels. Carries jewel tones and stark contrast well. Avoid muted, dusty, earthy tones.

beach wedding.text: Beach wedding: light, breathable fabrics (linen, chiffon), flowing silhouettes, soft/airy palette (pastels, sand, sky blue, coral). Flat or block-heel sandals (no stilettos in sand). Avoid heavy formal black-tie and stiff suits.

formal.text: Formal / black-tie: floor-length or elegant midi dresses, tailored suits, refined fabrics (silk, satin, wool). Deeper, richer colors; minimal but polished accessories. Elevated footwear.

cocktail.text: Cocktail / evening: knee-to-midi dresses or smart separates, a statement piece, heels or dressy flats. Jewel tones, metallics, or classic black read well at night.

interview.text: Interview / business: tailored, conservative pieces - blazer, structured trousers or pencil skirt, crisp shirt. Neutral, low-contrast palette (navy, grey, charcoal, white). Minimal accessories; clean closed-toe shoes.

casual.text: Casual / daytime: comfortable, relaxed fits - well-fitting jeans, knits, tees, sneakers or loafers. Mix a neutral base with one color pop. Keep it effortless and unfussy.

sport.text: Sport / active: performance fabrics, fitted activewear, supportive footwear. Functional layering. Bright or tonal athletic palettes both work.

pairing_rules.text: Build around a neutral base (black, white, navy, grey, beige, denim) and add one or two accent colors. Neutrals pair with almost anything.
Complementary pairs (opposites) create energy: blue+orange, purple+yellow, green+red. Use one as the dominant and the other as a small accent.
Tonal/monochrome looks (shades of one color) are elegant and elongating - vary the textures so it does not look flat.
When mixing patterns, vary the scale (one large, one small) and keep a shared color between them. Otherwise pair a pattern with solids.
